intro: Friday's defining story is Meta breaking from its open-source roots with Muse Spark — a closed, proprietary frontier model built for advertising and commerce. Meanwhile Anthropic's revenue surpasses OpenAI's for the first time, OpenAI's ChatGPT ad pilot crosses $100M annualised revenue, and Microsoft launches three in-house multimodal foundation models.

top_stories.headline: Meta Debuts Muse Spark — Its First Closed-Source AI Model, Built for Ads and Commerce
top_stories.summary: Meta launched Muse Spark on April 8–9, its first ever proprietary, closed-source frontier model — a sharp reversal from the Llama open-source strategy. Built by Meta Superintelligence Labs under Alexandr Wang, the model is natively multimodal with tool-use, visual chain-of-thought, and multi-agent orchestration. API access is invite-only now, with a self-serve launch expected later in 2026.

top_stories.headline: Anthropic's Revenue Run Rate Hits $30B ARR — Overtaking OpenAI at $24B
top_stories.summary: Anthropic's annualised revenue has crossed $30 billion, overtaking OpenAI's $24 billion ARR in a remarkable reversal driven by explosive enterprise demand. The company doubled its million-dollar-plus customers in under two months, with Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 leading in coding and agentic tasks. OpenAI's ARR dipped slightly from a $25B peak as GPT-5.4 rolls out to re-accelerate engagement.

top_stories.headline: OpenAI's ChatGPT Ad Pilot Crosses $100M Annualised Revenue in Six Weeks
top_stories.summary: OpenAI launched ads in ChatGPT in January 2026 and crossed $100 million in annualised revenue within six weeks, now with 600+ advertisers on a cost-per-engagement model. The company projects $2.5 billion in total ad revenue for 2026, scaling to $53 billion by 2029 — establishing advertising as a structural pillar of AI monetisation alongside subscriptions and API revenue.

top_stories.headline: Microsoft Launches Three Proprietary Multimodal Foundation Models: MAI-Transcribe-1, MAI-Voice-1, MAI-Image-2
top_stories.summary: Microsoft released its first full in-house multimodal model suite on April 2, competing directly with OpenAI, Google, and ElevenLabs. MAI-Transcribe-1 supports 25 languages at a 3.9% error rate beating Gemini Flash and GPT-Transcribe; MAI-Voice-1 generates 60 seconds of audio in one second; MAI-Image-2 is 2x faster than its predecessor. All three are available through Microsoft AI Foundry.

top_stories.headline: OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Form Intelligence-Sharing Pact Against Chinese AI Distillation Attacks
top_stories.summary: Through the Frontier Model Forum, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google announced formal threat-intelligence sharing to detect and report Chinese-linked model extraction campaigns. Anthropic previously accused DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax of generating 16 million fake exchanges via ~24,000 fraudulent accounts to copy Claude's capabilities. Google's Threat Intelligence Group separately disrupted a Gemini extraction campaign involving over 100,000 prompts.

quick_hits.text: Perplexity's ARR jumped from ~$300M to ~$450M in a single month after launching Perplexity Computer, an autonomous AI agent product — monthly active users now exceed 100 million.
Anthropic launched Claude Managed Agents, a composable API suite enabling developers to build and deploy scalable cloud agents without managing underlying infrastructure.
OpenClaw, an open-source AI agent framework originating in China, surpassed 302,000 GitHub stars — the fastest-growing open-source project in GitHub history.

Microsoft open-sourced its Agent Governance Toolkit providing security protection for AI agents against 10 critical attack vectors including prompt injection and SSRF — as 97% of enterprises say they expect a major AI agent security incident this year.

Anthropic's Model Context Protocol (MCP) crossed 97 million installs in March 2026 under the newly formed Agentic AI Foundation within the Linux Foundation, alongside OpenAI's AGENTS.md and Block's goose framework.

india_roundup.text: India ranked #1 among 15 countries in active AI use for strategic decision-making in Deloitte's April 2026 report — 40% of Indian enterprises report significant or full AI usage vs. a 28% global average — but ranks near the bottom on deep technical AI expertise.
Global AI investment has crossed $800 billion with India identified as a primary applications hub — second only to the US in enterprise AI adoption velocity, per a Business Standard report.

AI governance is becoming a C-suite priority in India: 94% of Indian organisations expect AI spend to increase over the next year, and the IndiaAI Mission's Rs 10,371 crore budget is shaping how enterprises approach responsible deployment.

IndiaAI Mission's Innovation Challenge 2026 invites market-deployed startups to compete for two-year government work contracts worth up to INR 1 crore to deploy AI solutions within government ministries.

Microsoft's $17.5 billion India AI infrastructure commitment is moving into execution — its new Hyderabad hyperscale cloud region goes live in mid-2026, and the company doubled its AI skilling target to 20 million Indians by 2030.

closing_insight: Meta's pivot from open-source to proprietary AI isn't just a product decision — it's a declaration that the open-source era served its purpose of building developer goodwill, and the monetisation era is now open. When the world's largest ad platform closes its models, the race for distribution advantage becomes the defining contest of the next AI chapter.
